codeReview.summary: The synthesis-reviewer successfully collected all 12 findings files from .factory/reviews/, deduplicated overlapping findings, and produced a comprehensive REVIEW_REPORT.md with 162 findings (0 Critical, 17 High, 57 Medium, 45 Low, 43 Info). The report includes proper sections organized by severity, cross-cutting patterns, recommendations, and validation contract assertions. The implementation fully covers the feature description and expected behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The synthesis-reviewer skill's procedure accurately matches what the worker implemented. Skill requires collecting findings, deduplicating, reviewing severity consistency, organizing by severity/category, identifying cross-cutting patterns, writing REVIEW_REPORT.md, and writing all_findings_summary.json - all completed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows whatWasImplemented matches skill procedure exactly. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that cross-cut reviews and module reviews overlap significantly (8 overlapping findings). This pattern should be documented in the synthesis-reviewer skill or library as guidance for future synthesis tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: all_findings_summary.json overlap_deduplication section shows 8 overlapping issues between module and cross-cut reviews with specific deduplication actions taken.

VAL-REPORT-001.assertion: REVIEW_REPORT.md exists with sections for Critical, High, Medium, Low findings
VAL-REPORT-001.evidence: REVIEW_REPORT.md exists (591 lines), contains Executive Summary, Critical Findings (none), High Findings (17), Medium Findings (57), Low Findings (45), Info Findings (43), Cross-Cutting Patterns, Recommendations sections

VAL-REPORT-002.assertion: All findings have file paths and actionable recommendations
VAL-REPORT-002.evidence: Sample review of High findings shows all have file paths (e.g., 'crates/liteclip-core/src/encode/ffmpeg/mod.rs'), location/line references, issue description, impact, and fix recommendation. Medium findings use table format with file and issue columns.

VAL-REPORT-003.assertion: Severity definitions documented, findings appropriately categorized
VAL-REPORT-003.evidence: Executive Summary table defines severity: Critical (crashes, data loss, security vulnerabilities), High (significant bugs, architectural flaws, resource leaks). Severity breakdown shows 0 Critical, 17 High, 57 Medium, 45 Low, 43 Info - appropriate distribution.

VAL-REPORT-004.assertion: Cross-cutting patterns section identifies systemic issues
VAL-REPORT-004.evidence: Cross-Cutting Patterns section identifies 6 systemic issues: FFmpeg unsafe blocks lacking SAFETY documentation, Thread join without timeout, Critical channel sends discarded, Mutex contention in hot paths, Windows API error handling via .ok(), Audio decode memory allocation. Also identifies 5 positive patterns.

overlaps.action: Kept encode version, merged impact details
Kept platform version with security context
Kept output version, added error handling context

overlaps.issue: Decode pipeline spin loop on full channel
overlaps.action: Kept gui version, merged threading implications
Kept gui version with performance category

overlaps.issue: Config load silent fallback to defaults

overlaps.action: Kept config version with security impact

deduplicationCheck.assessment: Deduplication performed correctly - overlapping findings merged appropriately keeping more detailed version while preserving context from both sources.

userReportedIssuesAnalysis.originalReport: some excess cpu and memory usage, some related to the ui
rootCausesIdentified: gui/decode_pipeline: Audio preload memory (full track decoded into memory)
gui/decode_pipeline: Spin loop with 1ms sleep on full frame channel
gui/decode_pipeline: Multiple Mutex acquisitions per playback frame
gui/decode_pipeline: Frame pool allocates ~24-40MB per video editor
gui: Thread spawning per video (decoder, audio stream, audio preload)
userReportedIssuesAnalysis.assessment: Worker correctly traced user-reported CPU/memory issues to specific code locations in GUI decode pipeline with clear root cause analysis.

positiveObservations: Comprehensive coverage - all 12 findings files collected and synthesized
Clear deduplication logic - overlapping findings merged intelligently
Well-structured report - Executive summary, severity sections, cross-cutting patterns, recommendations
User-reported issues properly traced to root causes
Validation contract assertions explicitly addressed in all_findings_summary.json
Cross-cutting patterns section provides actionable systemic insights
summary: PASS. The synthesize-final-report feature successfully completed all expected behavior. REVIEW_REPORT.md (591 lines) contains comprehensive findings organized by severity with actionable recommendations. all_findings_summary.json documents deduplication of 8 overlapping findings from 173 raw findings to 162 deduplicated. All 4 validation contract assertions (VAL-REPORT-001 through VAL-REPORT-004) are satisfied. User-reported CPU/memory issues are traced to specific GUI decode pipeline root causes.
